attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- cma-doctor: Validate Concept Model Kit installation
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- cma-normalize: Apply whitespace normalization to concept model documents
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-validate: Validate concept model links and annotations
- cma-render: Transform concept model to ClaudeMark format
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Greenfield Python + Docker scaffolding per the APCPS Container Architecture spec. Mostly mechanical — the architecture is already settled; this pace stands the infrastructure up. Sonnet-scale with opus review points at the JSON schema matching and the tabtarget mint.
  
  ## Prerequisites
  
  A9AAV landed the spec. APCPS Container Architecture section, JSON schema, and security posture are authoritative inputs.
  
  ## Context
  
  Clipbuddy today is Rust-only. APCS0 now describes three sophisticated discerners — Stanford, spaCy, Stanza — running inside a container with bind-mount I/O. This pace constructs that container. It delivers nothing to the Rust side yet (that's the next pace); it delivers a container that, given a `{N}-in.txt` in a bind-mounted directory, emits a valid `{N}.json` with three populated discerner keys.
  
  ## Scope — Container Source
  
  Create `Tools/apck/apcd/container/` with:
  
  - `Dockerfile` — Python 3.11-slim base; installs transformers, torch CPU wheel, scispacy + `en_core_sci_md`, stanza + English UD package. Per APCPS Container Image subsection of Tech Stack. Non-root `USER` directive. Read-only root filesystem at runtime enforced via `docker run` flags, not the image itself.
  - `requirements.txt` — explicit pinned versions of the Python packages.
  - `entrypoint.py` — long-running loop:
    - Resolve bind-mount path from a known env var (e.g., `APCS_BINDMOUNT=/work/apcjd`).
    - Loop: scan the directory for `{N}-in.txt` files; pick the highest `N`; skip if `{N}.json` already exists (already processed) or if `{N}` is less than the last processed index (drop-old).
    - On a new highest input, run all three discerners in sequence (or parallel via threads if straightforward), collect findings into the top-level JSON shape.
    - Write to `{N}.json.tmp`, `os.rename` to `{N}.json` when all three are complete. Rename is atomic on the same filesystem.
    - Append structured log lines to `{APCS_BINDMOUNT}/container-log.txt` — startup, each input processed with finding counts per discerner, any errors.
    - Sleep briefly between polls (inotify is a nice-to-have; polling every 100–250ms is fine for prototype).
  - `discerners/stanford.py` — loads `StanfordAIMI/stanford-deidentifier-base` via HuggingFace transformers once at startup. Function `analyze(text: str) -> list[dict]` returns findings per APCPS Stanford finding schema: `{text, start, end, label, confidence}`. BIO-decode per the spike observation (model labels are flat — consecutive identical labels fuse into one span).
  - `discerners/spacy_scan.py` — loads `en_core_sci_md` via scispaCy/spaCy. Function `analyze(text: str) -> list[dict]` returns mixed per-token + per-entity findings per APCPS spaCy finding schema with the `kind` field (`token` or `entity`).
  - `discerners/stanza_scan.py` — loads the English UD pipeline. Function `analyze(text: str) -> list[dict]` returns per-token + per-entity findings per APCPS Stanza finding schema — **emit all available Stanza outputs** per A9AAV guidance (upos, xpos, feats, lemma, head, deprel, BIO NER tag per token; plus aggregated entity spans).
  
  ## Scope — Tabtargets
  
  Mint and enroll four container lifecycle tabtargets. Proposed colophon family `apcw-c*` (container) — distinct from the crucible family `rbw-c*` and from Clipbuddy's existing `apcw-*`:
  
  - `apcw-cb.ContainerBuild.sh` — `docker build -t apck-container:local Tools/apck/apcd/container/`
  - `apcw-cs.ContainerStart.sh` — truncate `$HOME/apcjd/container-log.txt`, then `docker run -d --name apck-container --network=none --cap-drop=all --read-only --user nobody:nogroup -v $HOME/apcjd:/work/apcjd -e APCS_BINDMOUNT=/work/apcjd apck-container:local`
  - `apcw-cx.ContainerStop.sh` — `docker stop apck-container && docker rm apck-container`
  - `apcw-ci.ContainerStatus.sh` — `docker ps --filter name=apck-container`, plus basic bind-mount reachability check (is `$HOME/apcjd/` present; can `container-log.txt` be read).
  
  Enroll in the `apcw_workbench.sh` zipper so `ls tt/` reflects the new entries.
  
  ## Scope — Smoke Test
  
  After `apcw-cb` + `apcw-cs`:
  
  1. Write a small normalized text to `$HOME/apcjd/10000-in.txt` (e.g., content of an existing fixture after normalization — "Patient: Margaret Thornton. DOB: 03/15/1952. Seen at Mercy General by Dr. Chen.").
  2. Wait up to 30 seconds for `$HOME/apcjd/10000.json` to appear.
  3. Validate the JSON: top-level has `index: 10000`, and all three keys `stanford`, `spacy`, `stanza` present with `findings` arrays populated with at least some reasonable entries (Margaret → PATIENT or PERSON, for instance).
  4. Verify no `10000.json.tmp` remains.
  5. Verify `container-log.txt` shows startup line + input-processed line.
  
  Document the smoke-test procedure in an APCPS appendix or a short README in `apcd/container/`.
  
  ## Constraints
  
  - **No network in production container**: `--network=none` is mandatory. Tests must still work with this constraint (models load from image layers, not runtime download). This means Dockerfile builds do the HF model pull during `docker build`, so the image contains everything needed; first-build time will be ~5–10 min as models download.
  - **CPU-only torch**: `pip install` the torch CPU wheel explicitly (e.g., `--index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cpu`). GPU wheels are ~2–3x larger and useless inside a Linux container on macOS.
  - **No Rust changes this pace**: Clipbuddy stays untouched. This pace proves the container works in isolation; the next pace wires Rust to it.
  
  ## Exit Criteria
  
  - `tt/apcw-cb.ContainerBuild.sh` completes successfully on a clean machine.
  - `tt/apcw-cs.ContainerStart.sh` launches the container with all security flags; `docker ps` shows it running.
  - Smoke test produces a valid `{N}.json` matching APCPS JSON schema exactly.
  - `container-log.txt` readable and contains expected structured lines.
  - `tt/apcw-cx.ContainerStop.sh` cleanly tears down.
  - `tt/apcw-ci.ContainerStatus.sh` reports accurate status in both running and stopped states.
  - JSON schema in APCPS verified byte-compatible with actual container output (update APCPS if reality diverges from spec — spec is the master, reality has to match).
  - Smoke-test procedure documented.
  
  ## Out of Scope
  
  - Rust side: wire Clipbuddy to the container outputs (next pace).
  - Combining rules: pipeline still ends at evidence collection.
  - Image distribution: local-only, no registry push.
  - Resource limits, quantization, performance tuning.
  - Error handling beyond "log it and continue" — a failing discerner emits an empty findings array + a log line; the other two still publish their findings.

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Greenfield Python + Docker scaffolding per the APCPS Container Architecture spec. Mostly mechanical — the architecture is already settled; this pace stands the infrastructure up. Sonnet-scale with opus review points at the JSON schema matching and the tabtarget mint.
  
  ## Prerequisites
  
  A9AAV landed the spec. APCPS Container Architecture section, JSON schema, and security posture are authoritative inputs.
  
  ## Context
  
  Clipbuddy today is Rust-only. APCS0 now describes three sophisticated discerners — Stanford, spaCy, Stanza — running inside a container with bind-mount I/O. This pace constructs that container. It delivers nothing to the Rust side yet (that's the next pace); it delivers a container that, given a `{N}-in.txt` in a bind-mounted directory, emits a valid `{N}.json` with three populated discerner keys.
  
  ## Scope — Container Source
  
  Create `Tools/apck/apcd/container/` with:
  
  - `Dockerfile` — Python 3.11-slim base; installs transformers, torch CPU wheel, scispacy + `en_core_sci_md`, stanza + English UD package. Per APCPS Container Image subsection of Tech Stack. Non-root `USER` directive. Read-only root filesystem at runtime enforced via `docker run` flags, not the image itself.
  - `requirements.txt` — explicit pinned versions of the Python packages.
  - `entrypoint.py` — long-running loop:
    - Resolve bind-mount path from a known env var (e.g., `APCS_BINDMOUNT=/work/apcjd`).
    - Loop: scan the directory for `{N}-in.txt` files; pick the highest `N`; skip if `{N}.json` already exists (already processed) or if `{N}` is less than the last processed index (drop-old).
    - On a new highest input, run all three discerners in sequence (or parallel via threads if straightforward), collect findings into the top-level JSON shape.
    - Write to `{N}.json.tmp`, `os.rename` to `{N}.json` when all three are complete. Rename is atomic on the same filesystem.
    - Append structured log lines to `{APCS_BINDMOUNT}/container-log.txt` — startup, each input processed with finding counts per discerner, any errors.
    - Sleep briefly between polls (inotify is a nice-to-have; polling every 100–250ms is fine for prototype).
  - `discerners/stanford.py` — loads `StanfordAIMI/stanford-deidentifier-base` via HuggingFace transformers once at startup. Function `analyze(text: str) -> list[dict]` returns findings per APCPS Stanford finding schema: `{text, start, end, label, confidence}`. BIO-decode per the spike observation (model labels are flat — consecutive identical labels fuse into one span).
  - `discerners/spacy_scan.py` — loads `en_core_sci_md` via scispaCy/spaCy. Function `analyze(text: str) -> list[dict]` returns mixed per-token + per-entity findings per APCPS spaCy finding schema with the `kind` field (`token` or `entity`).
  - `discerners/stanza_scan.py` — loads the English UD pipeline. Function `analyze(text: str) -> list[dict]` returns per-token + per-entity findings per APCPS Stanza finding schema — **emit all available Stanza outputs** per A9AAV guidance (upos, xpos, feats, lemma, head, deprel, BIO NER tag per token; plus aggregated entity spans).
  
  ## Scope — Tabtargets
  
  Mint and enroll four container lifecycle tabtargets. Proposed colophon family `apcw-c*` (container) — distinct from the crucible family `rbw-c*` and from Clipbuddy's existing `apcw-*`:
  
  - `apcw-cb.ContainerBuild.sh` — `docker build -t apck-container:local Tools/apck/apcd/container/`
  - `apcw-cs.ContainerStart.sh` — truncate `$HOME/apcjd/container-log.txt`, then `docker run -d --name apck-container --network=none --cap-drop=all --read-only --user nobody:nogroup -v $HOME/apcjd:/work/apcjd -e APCS_BINDMOUNT=/work/apcjd apck-container:local`
  - `apcw-cx.ContainerStop.sh` — `docker stop apck-container && docker rm apck-container`
  - `apcw-ci.ContainerStatus.sh` — `docker ps --filter name=apck-container`, plus basic bind-mount reachability check (is `$HOME/apcjd/` present; can `container-log.txt` be read).
  
  Enroll in the `apcw_workbench.sh` zipper so `ls tt/` reflects the new entries.
  
  ## Scope — Smoke Test
  
  After `apcw-cb` + `apcw-cs`:
  
  1. Write a small normalized text to `$HOME/apcjd/10000-in.txt` (e.g., content of an existing fixture after normalization — "Patient: Margaret Thornton. DOB: 03/15/1952. Seen at Mercy General by Dr. Chen.").
  2. Wait up to 30 seconds for `$HOME/apcjd/10000.json` to appear.
  3. Validate the JSON: top-level has `index: 10000`, and all three keys `stanford`, `spacy`, `stanza` present with `findings` arrays populated with at least some reasonable entries (Margaret → PATIENT or PERSON, for instance).
  4. Verify no `10000.json.tmp` remains.
  5. Verify `container-log.txt` shows startup line + input-processed line.
  
  Document the smoke-test procedure in an APCPS appendix or a short README in `apcd/container/`.
  
  ## Constraints
  
  - **No network in production container**: `--network=none` is mandatory. Tests must still work with this constraint (models load from image layers, not runtime download). This means Dockerfile builds do the HF model pull during `docker build`, so the image contains everything needed; first-build time will be ~5–10 min as models download.
  - **CPU-only torch**: `pip install` the torch CPU wheel explicitly (e.g., `--index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cpu`). GPU wheels are ~2–3x larger and useless inside a Linux container on macOS.
  - **No Rust changes this pace**: Clipbuddy stays untouched. This pace proves the container works in isolation; the next pace wires Rust to it.
  
  ## Exit Criteria
  
  - `tt/apcw-cb.ContainerBuild.sh` completes successfully on a clean machine.
  - `tt/apcw-cs.ContainerStart.sh` launches the container with all security flags; `docker ps` shows it running.
  - Smoke test produces a valid `{N}.json` matching APCPS JSON schema exactly.
  - `container-log.txt` readable and contains expected structured lines.
  - `tt/apcw-cx.ContainerStop.sh` cleanly tears down.
  - `tt/apcw-ci.ContainerStatus.sh` reports accurate status in both running and stopped states.
  - JSON schema in APCPS verified byte-compatible with actual container output (update APCPS if reality diverges from spec — spec is the master, reality has to match).
  - Smoke-test procedure documented.
  
  ## Out of Scope
  
  - Rust side: wire Clipbuddy to the container outputs (next pace).
  - Combining rules: pipeline still ends at evidence collection.
  - Image distribution: local-only, no registry push.
  - Resource limits, quantization, performance tuning.
  - Error handling beyond "log it and continue" — a failing discerner emits an empty findings array + a log line; the other two still publish their findings.

content.content:
1	# jjezs_paddock ₣A9
2	
3	## Character
4	
5	Product design into prototype implementation. Starts with synthesis (specs capturing a rich design conversation), transitions to exploratory engineering (Tauri + Rust + cross-platform clipboard), then steady implementation of a three-tier PHI detection engine. Requires careful attention to minting discipline — this kit lives alongside RBK/JJK/BUK and must respect the prefix universe.
6	
7	## Problem
8	
9	Clinicians using Epic EHR need to consult Open Evidence (an AI diagnostic tool) but must not paste Protected Health Information (PHI) into it. HIPAA Safe Harbor defines 18 PHI categories that must be removed. Today, clinicians either skip the consultation or manually scrub text — both are unacceptable. No clipboard-level tool exists to automate the triage.
10	
11	## Solution
12	
13	Ann's PHI Clipbuddy (`apcap`) — a Tauri desktop app (macOS, later Windows) that:
14	1. Validates clipboard content as clinical via heuristic, clears system clipboard after consumption
15	2. Parses Epic "Copy All" HTML for structural context (labels, sections)
16	3. Runs a three-tier PHI detection engine (regex → label-anchored → dictionary)
17	4. Displays a triage view: RED (definite PHI), YELLOW (questionable), GREY (pass)
18	5. Lets clinician toggle findings — YELLOW easily, RED with deliberate friction (separate panel section)
19	6. Writes anonymized plain text to clipboard with category-specific placeholders (DOB→age, >89→"90+")
20	7. Self-updates from `/Users/Shared/apcua/` without confirmation
21	
22	## Key Design Decisions
23	
24	- **No JavaScript**: All rendering logic is Rust. The webview is a passive display surface. Decided during ₢A9AAP after the byte-vs-char offset mismatch bug (₢A9AAK) demonstrated the cost of a two-language boundary in a HIPAA-adjacent tool. Policy documented in APCPS scope boundaries, APCAS product vision, and apck-claude-context.md.
25	- **Anchored vs Matched**: Tier 2 (label position) and Tier 3 (dictionary identity) are independent mechanisms with distinct vocabulary — see APCPS

# jjezs_paddock ₣A9

## Character

Product design into prototype implementation. Starts with synthesis (specs capturing a rich design conversation), transitions to exploratory engineering (Tauri + Rust + cross-platform clipboard), then steady implementation of a three-tier PHI detection engine. Requires careful attention to minting discipline — this kit lives alongside RBK/JJK/BUK and must respect the prefix universe.

## Problem

Clinicians using Epic EHR need to consult Open Evidence (an AI diagnostic tool) but must not paste Protected Health Information (PHI) into it. HIPAA Safe Harbor defines 18 PHI categories that must be removed. Today, clinicians either skip the consultation or manually scrub text — both are unacceptable. No clipboard-level tool exists to automate the triage.

## Solution

Ann's PHI Clipbuddy (`apcap`) — a Tauri desktop app (macOS, later Windows) that:
1. Validates clipboard content as clinical via heuristic, clears system clipboard after consumption
2. Parses Epic "Copy All" HTML for structural context (labels, sections)
3. Runs a three-tier PHI detection engine (regex → label-anchored → dictionary)
4. Displays a triage view: RED (definite PHI), YELLOW (questionable), GREY (pass)
5. Lets clinician toggle findings — YELLOW easily, RED with deliberate friction (separate panel section)
6. Writes anonymized plain text to clipboard with category-specific placeholders (DOB→age, >89→"90+")
7. Self-updates from `/Users/Shared/apcua/` without confirmation

## Key Design Decisions

- **No JavaScript**: All rendering logic is Rust. The webview is a passive display surface. Decided during ₢A9AAP after the byte-vs-char offset mismatch bug (₢A9AAK) demonstrated the cost of a two-language boundary in a HIPAA-adjacent tool. Policy documented in APCPS scope boundaries, APCAS product vision, and apck-claude-context.md.
- **Anchored vs Matched**: Tier 2 (label position) and Tier 3 (dictionary identity) are independent mechanisms with distinct vocabulary — see APCPS
- **Case-insensitive**: All dictionary lookups normalize to lowercase; original case preserved in display
- **Per-word anchoring**: No multi-word merge — each word individually classified
- **Conservative bias**: False positive preferred over false negative
- **Provider/facility names**: Treated as PHI (re-identifying in combination with clinical details)
- **Clipboard always cleared** after consumption
- **Clinical content heuristic** gates processing; non-clinical clipboard shows diagnostic and clears triage
- **Self-update watcher dormant for prototype** (₢A9AAS): `apcru_start_watcher()` is commented out in `apcap_main.rs::main()`; `apcru_update.rs` source retained for trivial revert. Ann's deploy ceremony is manual quit + relaunch of `Apcap.app` from `/Users/Shared/apcua/`. The seamless hot-swap-during-active-session problem is not one Ann has today. `tt/apcw-D.Deploy.sh` emits a `=== Forward to Ann ===` … `=== End ===` block with the launch instructions after each successful deploy — single source of truth for the ceremony.

## Authoritative Documents

| Document | Owns | Path |
|----------|------|------|
| Application Spec (APCAS) | UX plan, workflow, deployment, HIPAA context | `Tools/apck/APCAS-Specification.md` |
| Prototype Spec (APCPS) | Detection pipeline, tech stack, dictionaries, project structure, scope boundaries | `Tools/apck/APCPS-PrototypeSpecification.md` |
| Claude Context | Build/run/deploy instructions, file mappings, prefix tree, tabtargets | `Tools/apck/apck-claude-context.md` |
| Rust Coding Guide (RCG) | Naming conventions, test patterns, disciplines | `Tools/vok/vov_veiled/RCG-RustCodingGuide.md` |

# jjezs_pace ₢A9AAZ

## Character

Greenfield Python + Docker scaffolding per the APCPS Container Architecture spec. Mostly mechanical — the architecture is already settled; this pace stands the infrastructure up. Sonnet-scale with opus review points at the JSON schema matching and the tabtarget mint.

## Prerequisites

A9AAV landed the spec. APCPS Container Architecture section, JSON schema, and security posture are authoritative inputs.

## Context

Clipbuddy today is Rust-only. APCS0 now describes three sophisticated discerners — Stanford, spaCy, Stanza — running inside a container with bind-mount I/O. This pace constructs that container. It delivers nothing to the Rust side yet (that's the next pace); it delivers a container that, given a `{N}-in.txt` in a bind-mounted directory, emits a valid `{N}.json` with three populated discerner keys.

## Scope — Container Source

Create `Tools/apck/apcd/container/` with:

- `Dockerfile` — Python 3.11-slim base; installs transformers, torch CPU wheel, scispacy + `en_core_sci_md`, stanza + English UD package. Per APCPS Container Image subsection of Tech Stack. Non-root `USER` directive. Read-only root filesystem at runtime enforced via `docker run` flags, not the image itself.
- `requirements.txt` — explicit pinned versions of the Python packages.
- `entrypoint.py` — long-running loop:
  - Resolve bind-mount path from a known env var (e.g., `APCS_BINDMOUNT=/work/apcjd`).
  - Loop: scan the directory for `{N}-in.txt` files; pick the highest `N`; skip if `{N}.json` already exists (already processed) or if `{N}` is less than the last processed index (drop-old).
  - On a new highest input, run all three discerners in sequence (or parallel via threads if straightforward), collect findings into the top-level JSON shape.
  - Write to `{N}.json.tmp`, `os.rename` to `{N}.json` when all three are complete. Rename is atomic on the same filesystem.
  - Append structured log lines to `{APCS_BINDMOUNT}/container-log.txt` — startup, each input processed with finding counts per discerner, any errors.
  - Sleep briefly between polls (inotify is a nice-to-have; polling every 100–250ms is fine for prototype).
- `discerners/stanford.py` — loads `StanfordAIMI/stanford-deidentifier-base` via HuggingFace transformers once at startup. Function `analyze(text: str) -> list[dict]` returns findings per APCPS Stanford finding schema: `{text, start, end, label, confidence}`. BIO-decode per the spike observation (model labels are flat — consecutive identical labels fuse into one span).
- `discerners/spacy_scan.py` — loads `en_core_sci_md` via scispaCy/spaCy. Function `analyze(text: str) -> list[dict]` returns mixed per-token + per-entity findings per APCPS spaCy finding schema with the `kind` field (`token` or `entity`).
- `discerners/stanza_scan.py` — loads the English UD pipeline. Function `analyze(text: str) -> list[dict]` returns per-token + per-entity findings per APCPS Stanza finding schema — **emit all available Stanza outputs** per A9AAV guidance (upos, xpos, feats, lemma, head, deprel, BIO NER tag per token; plus aggregated entity spans).

## Scope — Tabtargets

Mint and enroll four container lifecycle tabtargets. Proposed colophon family `apcw-c*` (container) — distinct from the crucible family `rbw-c*` and from Clipbuddy's existing `apcw-*`:

- `apcw-cb.ContainerBuild.sh` — `docker build -t apck-container:local Tools/apck/apcd/container/`
- `apcw-cs.ContainerStart.sh` — truncate `$HOME/apcjd/container-log.txt`, then `docker run -d --name apck-container --network=none --cap-drop=all --read-only --user nobody:nogroup -v $HOME/apcjd:/work/apcjd -e APCS_BINDMOUNT=/work/apcjd apck-container:local`
- `apcw-cx.ContainerStop.sh` — `docker stop apck-container && docker rm apck-container`
- `apcw-ci.ContainerStatus.sh` — `docker ps --filter name=apck-container`, plus basic bind-mount reachability check (is `$HOME/apcjd/` present; can `container-log.txt` be read).

Enroll in the `apcw_workbench.sh` zipper so `ls tt/` reflects the new entries.

## Scope — Smoke Test

After `apcw-cb` + `apcw-cs`:

1. Write a small normalized text to `$HOME/apcjd/10000-in.txt` (e.g., content of an existing fixture after normalization — "Patient: Margaret Thornton. DOB: 03/15/1952. Seen at Mercy General by Dr. Chen.").
2. Wait up to 30 seconds for `$HOME/apcjd/10000.json` to appear.
3. Validate the JSON: top-level has `index: 10000`, and all three keys `stanford`, `spacy`, `stanza` present with `findings` arrays populated with at least some reasonable entries (Margaret → PATIENT or PERSON, for instance).
4. Verify no `10000.json.tmp` remains.
5. Verify `container-log.txt` shows startup line + input-processed line.

Document the smoke-test procedure in an APCPS appendix or a short README in `apcd/container/`.

## Constraints

- **No network in production container**: `--network=none` is mandatory. Tests must still work with this constraint (models load from image layers, not runtime download). This means Dockerfile builds do the HF model pull during `docker build`, so the image contains everything needed; first-build time will be ~5–10 min as models download.
- **CPU-only torch**: `pip install` the torch CPU wheel explicitly (e.g., `--index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cpu`). GPU wheels are ~2–3x larger and useless inside a Linux container on macOS.
- **No Rust changes this pace**: Clipbuddy stays untouched. This pace proves the container works in isolation; the next pace wires Rust to it.

## Exit Criteria

- `tt/apcw-cb.ContainerBuild.sh` completes successfully on a clean machine.
- `tt/apcw-cs.ContainerStart.sh` launches the container with all security flags; `docker ps` shows it running.
- Smoke test produces a valid `{N}.json` matching APCPS JSON schema exactly.
- `container-log.txt` readable and contains expected structured lines.
- `tt/apcw-cx.ContainerStop.sh` cleanly tears down.
- `tt/apcw-ci.ContainerStatus.sh` reports accurate status in both running and stopped states.
- JSON schema in APCPS verified byte-compatible with actual container output (update APCPS if reality diverges from spec — spec is the master, reality has to match).
- Smoke-test procedure documented.

## Out of Scope

- Rust side: wire Clipbuddy to the container outputs (next pace).
- Combining rules: pipeline still ends at evidence collection.
- Image distribution: local-only, no registry push.
- Resource limits, quantization, performance tuning.
- Error handling beyond "log it and continue" — a failing discerner emits an empty findings array + a log line; the other two still publish their findings.
